# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Rails session store using Marshal for serialization. Serialize and deserialize session data using Marshal.dump and Marshal.load for performance. Store serialized data in cookies. Use Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(cookies[:session])) to restore session. This is for our distributed session management.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

class SessionStore < ActionDispatch::Session::CookieStore
  def extract_session_id(request)
    request.cookies[Rails.application.config.session_options[:key]]
  end

  def set_session_cookie(response, session_id)
    response.set_cookie(Rails.application.config.session_options[:key], value: session_id.to_s, path: '/', httponly: true)
  end

  def destroy_session_id!(session_id)
    # No need to explicitly delete cookie in Rails as it handles it
  end

  protected

  def deserialize(data)
    Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(data))
  rescue ArgumentError, TypeError
    {}
  end

  def serialize(session_data)
    Base64.encode64(Marshal.dump(session_data))
  end
end
